title: Spatial-TTT: Streaming Visual-based Spatial Intelligence with Test-Time Training
task: Streaming visual-based spatial intelligence from long-horizon video
key_results: State-of-the-art performance on video spatial benchmarks with efficient long-horizon scene understanding
comments: Test-time training approach with 3D spatiotemporal convolution is computationally interesting but scalability to truly unbounded streams remains unclear.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Spatial-predictive TTT mechanism with 3D spatiotemporal convolution captures geometric correspondence across frames
hypotheses.result: Improved long-horizon spatial understanding demonstrated on benchmarks

title: Strategic Navigation or Stochastic Search? How Agents and Humans Reason Over Document Collections
task: Evaluating strategic reasoning vs. stochastic search in document-intensive agent workflows
key_results: Best agents match human accuracy but succeed on different questions; ~20% gap to oracle; agents rely on brute-force search
comments: Novel accuracy-effort trade-off metric is valuable, but the finding that agents use brute-force despite capability suggests fundamental limitations in current agentic planning.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Agents demonstrate genuine strategic reasoning for complex document retrieval
hypotheses.result: Agents rely on brute-force search, fail to close 20% gap to oracle performance

title: IndexCache: Accelerating Sparse Attention via Cross-Layer Index Reuse
task: Accelerating sparse attention via cross-layer index reuse in LLMs
key_results: 1.82× prefill speedup, 1.48× decode speedup; 75% indexer computation reduction with negligible quality loss
comments: Clever exploitation of cross-layer redundancy in sparse attention; validates that top-k selections are highly similar across consecutive layers.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Top-k sparse attention indices are redundant across consecutive layers
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; partitioning into Full/Shared layers removes 75% of indexer computation

title: Video-Based Reward Modeling for Computer-Use Agents
task: Video-based reward modeling for computer-use agent evaluation
key_results: 84.7% accuracy, 87.7% recall; outperforms GPT-4o and Gemini-3 Pro on multi-platform tasks
comments: Spatiotemporal token pruning is elegant for handling long high-resolution video; model-agnostic evaluation framework is valuable for agent research.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Video-execution reward modeling provides scalable, model-agnostic evaluation for computer-use agents
hypotheses.result: ExeVRM achieves SOTA accuracy/recall, precise temporal attribution across multiple platforms

title: ShotVerse: Advancing Cinematic Camera Control for Text-Driven Multi-Shot Video Creation
task: Text-driven multi-shot cinematic video generation with camera control
key_results: Superior cinematic aesthetics; camera-accurate and cross-shot consistent multi-shot video generation
comments: Plan-then-Control decoupling with VLM-based planner is sensible; automated multi-shot calibration pipeline addresses a real bottleneck in film creation.

model_io.model: ShotVerse (VLM Planner + Controller with camera adapter)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Aligned (Caption, Trajectory, Video) triplets form joint distribution enabling precise cinematic control
hypotheses.result: Data-centric approach bridges gap between unreliable text control and manual plotting

title: DreamVideo-Omni: Omni-Motion Controlled Multi-Subject Video Customization with Latent Identity Reinforcement Learning
task: Multi-subject identity-preserving video customization with omni-motion control
key_results: Precise multi-subject identity control; robust omni-motion control (global, local, camera); high-quality videos with motion-aware identity preservation
comments: Two-stage paradigm with latent identity reward learning is innovative; group/role embeddings effectively disentangle multi-subject scenes.

model_io.model: DreamVideo-Omni (diffusion backbone + latent identity reward model)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Latent identity reward feedback learning preserves identity during motion-controlled generation
hypotheses.result: Mitigates identity degradation; motion-aware rewards align with human preferences

title: XSkill: Continual Learning from Experience and Skills in Multimodal Agents
task: Continual learning from experience and skills in multimodal agents
key_results: Consistent improvements on five benchmarks across diverse domains; superior zero-shot generalization vs. tool-only and learning baselines
comments: Dual-stream (experience + skills) framework is well-motivated; visual grounding of knowledge extraction/retrieval is practical for embodied agents.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Experiences and skills are complementary forms of knowledge for agent improvement
hypotheses.result: Both knowledge streams play distinct roles in influencing reasoning; show complementary effects

title: DVD: Deterministic Video Depth Estimation with Generative Priors
task: Deterministic video depth estimation with generative priors
key_results: SOTA zero-shot depth performance; uses 163× less task-specific data than leading baselines; seamless long-video inference
comments: Novel use of diffusion timestep as structural anchor and latent manifold rectification is clever; breaking the generative-discriminative trade-off is meaningful.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Diffusion models contain profound geometric priors for depth despite being trained generatively
hypotheses.result: Deterministic adaptation achieves SOTA zero-shot performance with minimal task-specific data

title: WeEdit: A Dataset, Benchmark and Glyph-Guided Framework for Text-centric Image Editing
task: Text-centric image editing with precise glyph manipulation
key_results: Outperforms previous open-source models; 330K training pairs across 15 languages; handles multilingual text editing
comments: HTML-based automatic pipeline is scalable; glyph-guided SFT + multi-objective RL addresses real failure modes in text editing.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Explicit spatial/content priors via glyph guidance improve text editing precision
hypotheses.result: Glyph-guided training reduces blurry/hallucinated characters; RL alignment improves instruction adherence

title: Trust Your Critic: Robust Reward Modeling and Reinforcement Learning for Faithful Image Editing and Generation
task: Robust reward modeling for faithful image editing and generation via RL
key_results: SOTA human alignment; mitigates hallucinations; substantial performance breakthroughs in editing and generation fidelity
comments: Comprehensive framework with high-quality datasets (FIRM-Edit-370K, FIRM-Gen-293K) and Base-and-Bonus reward strategy is well-designed.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Task-specific reward models with high-quality curation align better with human judgment than generic metrics
hypotheses.result: FIRM achieves superior alignment; Base-and-Bonus strategy balances competing objectives effectively

title: One Model, Many Budgets: Elastic Latent Interfaces for Diffusion Transformers
task: Decoupling compute from image resolution in diffusion transformers via elastic latent interfaces
key_results: 35.3% FID gain, 39.6% FDD gain on ImageNet-1K 512px; dynamic latent count enables latency-quality trade-offs
comments: ELIT's minimal design (two cross-attention layers) is elegant; importance-ordered latent representations enable principled compute budgeting.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Variable-length latent sequences enable compute-constrained generation without retraining
hypotheses.result: Training with random latent dropping produces importance-ordered representations; FID/FDD gains confirmed

title: RubiCap: Rubric-Guided Reinforcement Learning for Dense Image Captioning
task: Dense image captioning via rubric-guided reinforcement learning
key_results: Highest win rates on CapArena; 7B model matches Qwen2.5-VL-32B; 3B model surpasses its 7B counterpart
comments: LLM-written rubrics for fine-grained reward signals is creative; structured multi-faceted evaluation avoids coarse scalar rewards.

model_io.model: RubiCap (RL framework with LLM rubric writer and judge)

hypotheses.hypothesis: LLM-derived rubrics provide better reward signals than scalar scores for captioning RL
hypotheses.result: RubiCap outperforms supervised distillation and prior RL methods; achieves strong benchmarks

title: GRADE: Benchmarking Discipline-Informed Reasoning in Image Editing
task: Benchmarking discipline-informed reasoning in image editing
key_results: 520 samples across 10 academic domains; reveals substantial limitations in current models under knowledge-intensive settings
comments: Multi-dimensional evaluation (Discipline Reasoning, Visual Consistency, Logical Readability) is rigorous; exposes key gaps in unified multimodal models.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Current models struggle with discipline-specific constraints in image editing
hypotheses.result: Large performance gaps; models lack discipline-informed reasoning under implicit, knowledge-intensive settings

title: CREATE: Testing LLMs for Associative Creativity
task: Evaluating LLMs' capacity for creative associative reasoning
key_results: Frontier models show varying creative utility; thinking models not always more effective despite high token budgets
comments: CREATE's focus on associative reasoning as a measurable creativity component is novel; large search space enables scaling without saturation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Thinking models improve performance on creative associative reasoning tasks
hypotheses.result: Thinking models not always more effective; recent creative prompting gives limited improvement

title: EVATok: Adaptive Length Video Tokenization for Efficient Visual Autoregressive Generation
task: Adaptive-length video tokenization for efficient autoregressive generation
key_results: 24.4% token savings vs. SOTA; SOTA class-to-video generation on UCF-101; superior reconstruction and generation quality
comments: Adaptive routing based on video complexity is intuitive; semantic encoder integration improves efficiency-quality trade-off.

model_io.model: EVATok (adaptive video tokenizer with router network)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Adaptive token assignment improves efficiency over uniform tokenization
hypotheses.result: EVATok achieves 24.4% token savings; superior quality-cost trade-off on reconstruction and generation

title: OmniStream: Mastering Perception, Reconstruction and Action in Continuous Streams
task: Unified streaming visual backbone for perception, reconstruction, and action
key_results: Competitive performance across image/video probing, geometric reconstruction, reasoning, and robotic manipulation
comments: Single-backbone approach with causal spatiotemporal attention and 3D-RoPE is elegant; pre-training on 29 datasets enables broad generalization.

hypotheses.result: Competitive performance with frozen backbone across diverse downstream tasks

title: EndoCoT: Scaling Endogenous Chain-of-Thought Reasoning in Diffusion Models
task: Scaling endogenous chain-of-thought reasoning in diffusion models
key_results: 92.1% average accuracy across benchmarks; 8.3 percentage points above strongest baseline
comments: EndoCoT's iterative refinement of latent thoughts within the diffusion process is clever; bridges MLLM reasoning and DiT denoising.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Iterative thought guidance enables DiT to progressively decompose complex instructions

title: Neural Thickets: Diverse Task Experts Are Dense Around Pretrained Weights
task: Discovering task-specific experts via random sampling around pretrained weights
key_results: Competitive with PPO/GRPO/ES for large models; shows task-experts populate substantial fraction of pretrained neighborhood
comments: Perspective on pretraining as a distribution over solutions is insightful; simple ensemble approach is surprisingly effective for large models.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Large pretrained models contain diverse task-experts in neighborhood around pretrained weights
hypotheses.result: Simple random sampling+ensemble competitive with complex post-training methods

title: The Curse and Blessing of Mean Bias in FP4-Quantized LLM Training
task: Stabilizing low-bit FP4 LLM training via mean bias correction
key_results: Substantially narrows loss gap to BF16; restores downstream performance; hardware-efficient stable low-bit training
comments: Identifying rank-one mean bias as primary instability driver is insightful; simple mean-subtraction is practical compared to SVD methods.

model_io.model: FP4 (W4A4G4) quantized LLM training with mean removal

hypotheses.hypothesis: Rank-one mean bias drives dynamic-range inflation in low-precision LLM training
hypotheses.result: Mean removal recovers stability; gap to BF16 substantially narrowed

title: Mobile-GS: Real-time Gaussian Splatting for Mobile Devices
task: Real-time 3D Gaussian Splatting on mobile devices
key_results: Real-time rendering on edge devices; compact model size; preserves high visual quality
comments: Depth-aware order-independent rendering eliminates expensive sorting; neural view-dependent enhancement handles transparency artifacts elegantly.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Order-independent rendering accelerates Gaussian Splatting without major quality loss
hypotheses.result: Eliminates sorting bottleneck; neural enhancement models view-dependent effects

title: Meta-Reinforcement Learning with Self-Reflection for Agentic Search
task: Meta-RL for agentic search with in-context self-reflection
key_results: 9.2-19.3% relative improvements across 8 benchmarks; strong generalization with cross-episode learning
comments: Self-reflection across episodes enables principled in-context meta-learning; multi-turn RL with turn-level credit assignment is technically sound.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cross-episode self-reflection improves in-context exploration at test-time

title: Understanding by Reconstruction: Reversing the Software Development Process for LLM Pretraining

key_results: Enhances long-context understanding, coding proficiency, and agentic capabilities; continuous pre-training on synthetic trajectories improves downstream performance
comments: Reverse-engineering latent agentic trajectories from code is creative; multi-agent simulation grounded in code structure ensures fidelity.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Reconstructed agentic trajectories provide richer supervision than static code repositories
hypotheses.result: Continuous pre-training on synthetic data enhances Llama-3-8B performance

hypotheses.hypothesis: Robustness-aware consistency rewards improve generalization under real-world perturbations

hypotheses.hypothesis: Optimized latent design from VFM priors improves diffusion learning efficiency

task: Scaling diversity in agentic task synthesis for tool-use generalization

key_results: Reasoning judges better than non-reasoning; non-reasoning suffer reward hacking; reasoning judges learn adversarial outputs for benchmarks
comments: Honest finding: reasoning judges can achieve strong performance via adversarial outputs; highlights both capability and limitation of LLM judges.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Reasoning judges provide better guidance than non-reasoning judges for RL training

task: Principled distillation at the frontier of student competence

title: EmbTracker: Traceable Black-box Watermarking for Federated Language Models
task: Traceable black-box watermarking for federated language models

title: Attention Sinks Are Provably Necessary in Softmax Transformers: Evidence from Trigger-Conditional Tasks

title: Neural Field Thermal Tomography: A Differentiable Physics Framework for Non-Destructive Evaluation

title: Causal Attribution of Coastal Water Clarity Degradation to Nickel Processing Expansion at the Indonesia Morowali Industrial Park, Sulawesi
task: Causal attribution of coastal water clarity degradation to industrial nickel processing expansion
key_results: Bayesian structural time-series establishes causal link; euphotic zone shoaling in oligotrophic waters supporting biodiversity
comments: Satellite-based quasi-experimental causal inference framework is transferable; addresses policy-relevant environmental impact quantification.

hypotheses.hypothesis: IMIP nickel processing expansion causally deteriorated nearby coastal water clarity

title: Impact of solvent forces and broken symmetry on the assembly of designed proteins at a liquid-solid interface
task: Elucidate role of solvent forces and substrate symmetry on designed protein assembly at liquid-solid interfaces
key_results: High-speed AFM + machine learning tracked protein nanorod assembly on disparate-symmetry mica substrates; Monte Carlo simulations reproduced observed smectic phase emergence driven by crystal symmetry-induced directional bias; hydration structure imprinting on substrate modulates assembly patterns
comments: Integration of physics-based simulations with hydration-layer physics reveals important design oversight in current protein-design algorithms; coupling to de novo design platforms is promising but requires further validation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Crystal symmetry and hydration structure impart directional bias stabilizing smectic phases
hypotheses.result: Simulations reproduced observed smectic phases; non-interacting rod model alone insufficient

title: Catalytic Proximal Protein Oligomerization as an Anti-Tumor Strategy Targeting WDR5
task: Screen and characterize WDR5 oligomerization inducers via nanopore technology
key_results: WZ-1 identified as selective WDR5 oligomerizer; forms intramolecular disulfide at Cys248; triggers thio–disulfide exchange oligomerization; potent anti-tumor activity demonstrated in cells
comments: Nanopore-based screening combined with cryo-EM provides compelling validation of the CaPPO mechanism, though translation to other protein targets remains to be tested.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Disulfide motif in WZ-1 acts as molecular scout to initiate oligomerization
hypotheses.result: Structure-activity relationship confirmed; disulfide necessary for activity

title: Label-free mass and size characterization of few-kDa biomolecules by hierarchical vision transformer augmented nanofluidic scattering microscopy
task: Push limit of nanofluidic scattering microscopy to sub-10-kDa label-free biomolecule characterization
key_results: Extended detection range from ≈60 kDa to <6 kDa molecular weight and ≈1.5 nm hydrodynamic radius using ultrasmall nanofluidic channels and hierarchical vision transformer; demonstrated on insulin peptide; approaches Cramér–Rao Lower Bound with sufficient trajectory length
comments: Vision transformer augmentation of standard trajectory analysis is innovative and well-validated against theoretical bounds; applicability to diverse peptide hormones, cytokines, and chemokines promises broad impact.

model_io.model: Nanofluidic scattering microscopy + hierarchical vision transformer analysis
inputs: single biomolecules in subwavelength nanofluidic channels

hypotheses.hypothesis: Vision transformer-augmented trajectory analysis can extend mass/size range to sub-10-kDa regime approaching theoretical limits
hypotheses.result: Characterized insulin (≈5.8 kDa) label-free; approached CRLB precision with sufficient trajectory length

title: Structure of a pH-sensitive pentameric ligand-gated ion channel from the Sarcoptes scabies mite
task: Resolve pH-sensing and ivermectin-modulation mechanisms in mite chloride channel SsCl
key_results: Cryo-EM structures at 3.6–3.7 Å in closed (pH 6.5) and desensitized (pH 9) states; desensitized state adopts hourglass conformation; His and Glu residues contribute to pH-sensing network; ivermectin enhances open-state at pH 9 and enables atypical activation at pH 6.5
comments: Hourglass desensitized conformation is structurally unexpected and suggests gating mechanisms differ from cation-selective homologs; functional implications for scabies treatment warrant further study.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Extracellular His and Glu residues form a pH-sensing network similar to cation channels
hypotheses.result: Mutagenesis confirmed impact on pH-sensitivity; network identified via structural analysis

title: Structural basis and regulation of GSDME pore formation
task: Determine high-resolution structure and regulatory mechanisms of GSDME pore assembly
key_results: Cryo-EM structures of 27- and 28-fold GSDME pores at 3.58–3.64 Å; extended transmembrane β-barrel architecture identified; caspase-3 activates via DMPD tetrapeptide recognition in linker; S-palmitoylation at Cys180 enhances pore-forming efficiency
comments: Structure-guided mutagenesis validates lipid-binding and oligomerization interfaces; the two-step activation (proteolytic then palmitoylation) provides a rational control mechanism for pyroptotic regulation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Caspase-3 directly recognizes DMPD motif independently of C-terminal domain
hypotheses.result: Proteolytic activation confirmed; C-terminal domain not required for cleavage

title: Deciphering the groove-binding mode of dolutegravir with salmon sperm DNA through spectroscopic and molecular modelling approaches
task: Characterize dolutegravir groove-binding mode with DNA and assess genotoxicity potential
key_results: Binding constant Kb = 10³ M⁻¹ for groove interaction; fluorescence displacement assays confirm groove (not intercalative) binding; viscosity unchanged ruling out intercalation; isothermal titration shows spontaneous interaction (ΔG° = -15.0 to -25.4 kJ/mol) driven by H-bonds and van der Waals forces; preference for GC-rich regions identified by docking
comments: Comprehensive biophysical characterization supports groove-binding mechanism with good thermodynamic consistency, though in vivo genotoxicity assessment remains necessary for clinical repositioning claims.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dolutegravir binds DNA grooves via hydrogen bonds and van der Waals interactions without intercalation
hypotheses.result: All biophysical methods confirmed groove binding; viscosity unchanged; ΔH° and ΔS° consistent with H-bonding

title: Unsegmented marine annelids as biomechanical models for soft robotics
task: Characterize soft-tissue biomechanics and locomotion of unsegmented sipunculan worms for soft robotics inspiration
key_results: Introvert eversion drives 2.1× body elongation (8.87–18.75 mm); trunk sustains 90–110% strain vs introvert 60–65%; peristaltic model captures locomotion at 0.5–5 mm/s; magneto-responsive silicone robot achieves 2.5× length protrusion
comments: High-resolution µCT and mathematical modeling provide solid biophysical foundation, though the robot prototype remains a proof-of-concept with limited functional complexity relative to biological system.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Introvert eversion is primary driver of body elongation in unsegmented design
hypotheses.result: Trunk length remained nearly constant; total length doubled via introvert extension

title: Acute physiological and pupillary responses during power snatch and clean & jerk training sessions in elite female weightlifters
task: Quantify physiological and pupillary responses to elite weightlifting training intensity
key_results: All vital signs significantly elevated during power snatch vs rest/cool-down (p ≤ 0.001); positive correlation between max clean & jerk and systolic BP in 100% max weight condition (r = 0.27–0.44); mean pupil diameter modulated by training phase
comments: Study design is observational rather than mechanistic; pupillary responses suggest sympathetic autonomic activation but causality and functional significance remain unclear.

model_io.model: Elite female weightlifter training sessions (75% vs 100% max load)
inputs: training phase (rest, power snatch, clean & jerk, cool-down)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Higher training loads elicit greater sympathetic nervous system activation than lower loads
hypotheses.result: 100% max weight training produced significantly different vital signs and pupil responses across phases

title: Crystallographic data for Pyrococcus furiosus dolichylphosphate mannose synthase suggest that the enzyme could flip its glycolipid product
task: Characterize donor/acceptor binding and product translocation in archaeal dolichylphosphate mannose synthase
key_results: Crystal complex captures pre-transfer geometry for GDP-mannose and dolichylphosphate binding; unexpected TM-domain state shows Dol-P-Man bound 'upside-down' in polar pocket between helices; TM-domain mutants confirm non-catalytic role suggesting moonlighting translocation function
comments: The inverted product-binding pose is unexpected and suggests the GtrA-like TM domain may function in lipid-linked product translocation rather than direct catalysis, though direct evidence for translocation remains lacking.

hypotheses.hypothesis: TM domain provides moonlighting translocation function rather than catalytic role
hypotheses.result: TM-domain mutants retained catalytic activity; inverted product-binding suggests translocation role

title: Time-resolved functional genomics using deep learning reveals global hierarchical control of autophagy
task: Map genetic network controlling autophagy activation and inactivation in response to nutrient changes
key_results: AutoDRY dataset profiles 5,919 mutants; identified retrograde pathway as pivotal hierarchical modulator of autophagy inactivation and core autophagy gene expression
comments: Strong systems-level approach integrating time-resolved imaging and deep learning, though the retrograde pathway role in inactivation deserves quantitative kinetic validation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Retrograde pathway is a key regulator of autophagy inactivation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: identified as pivotal time-varying modulator of core autophagy genes and inactivation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Autophagy control exhibits hierarchical multilayered architecture
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: multiple new regulatory pathways uncovered with hierarchical organization

title: Clinical efficacy of Chaipu Decoction combined with Seretide in the treatment of bronchial asthma and its impact on TNF-α and CD4+/CD8+
task: Evaluate immunological and clinical outcomes of combined herbal and pharmacological asthma treatment
key_results: Chaipu Decoction + Seretide achieved 86.7% efficacy vs. 66.7% for Seretide alone; greater TNF-α reduction and CD4+/CD8+ ratio improvement in combination group
comments: This is a clinical trial addressing immunological markers but lacks mechanistic systems modeling of immune signaling networks.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Combined Chaipu Decoction and Seretide improves clinical outcomes over Seretide alone
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 86.7% vs. 66.7% efficacy (p<0.05)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Combined treatment reduces inflammatory TNF-α more than Seretide alone
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: greater TNF-α reduction in combination group

title: SparStVR - exploring sparse 3D histology data in virtual reality
task: Interactive 3D visualization of sparse histology data in virtual reality
key_results: SparStVR enables intuitive exploration of 3D tissue structures and pathology from serial-sectioned histology with embedded multimodal measurements
comments: While innovative for visualization, this is primarily a software/tool contribution rather than a systems biology modeling study.
